comment_text.sn34.4:1.4: Kallita means “healthy and happy, well-disposed”, and is explained by the commentary as “gladdening the mind”. Any meditator knows that there are ups and downs in the process, and it is essential to keep the mind balanced, with a positive attitude.

root_text.sn34.4:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo.
root_text.sn34.4:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.
root_text.sn34.4:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo.
root_text.sn34.4:1.7: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo ca.
root_text.sn34.4:1.8: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

translation_text.sn34.4:1.4: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in positivity for immersion. …”
